mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates relative to its recent level, revenue momentum improves for consumer-facing firms and the market should price higher near-term earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive versus the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS acceleration is not associated with above-median 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional mean is nonpositive for any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in INDPRO and HOUST indicates broad supply-side activity and industrial throughput, which should support cyclical equities and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-slope condition on INDPRO and HOUST fails to outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of jointly-positive episodes.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL inflation decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness eases and equity risk premia compress, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate falls for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation episodes do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the conditional success rate stays below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market weakening that should reduce earnings visibility and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not predict negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which should raise S&P 500 prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP growth fails to align with positive 60-day SPX returns in out-of-sample testing, or if the sign of the conditional mean reverses for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, real rates are deeply negative and the market can sustain risk-on pricing, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate/high-inflation regime does not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction condition is triggered without a positive average return for any 12-month window.
